title: Notogrammitis angustifolia
taxonomy: Notogrammitis angustifolia belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. It is classified under the phylum Marchantiophyta, a division of non-vascular plants. The genus Notogrammitis is further categorized within the class Polypodiopsida, which includes various species of ferns. Notogrammitis angustifolia specifically falls under the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns characterized by their unique leaf structure. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Notogrammitis angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved Notogrammitis, is a species of fern native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Notogrammitis angustifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Notogrammitis angustifolia is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used topically to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
